Off Calendar Jan-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kathleen B. Ebrahimi of POND NORTH LLP (415-217-1240), appearing for defendant / moving party Fraser's Boiler Service, Inc.
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Harold Simmons

RULING:
DEFENDANT FRASER'S BOILER SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Fraser's Boiler Service, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff's claims are barred by the statute of limitations. (C.C.P. 340.2.) Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether his claim is barred by the statute of limitations. Plaintiff suffered a disability as defined by C.C.P. 340.2 when he had to stop working on and sell his avocado grove. More than one year passed from the time plaintiff knew, or in the exercise of reasonable diligence should have known, that his disability was caused by exposure to asbestos and the filing of his complaint. The Court further finds that plaintiff Harold Simmons' declaration conflicts with his sworn deposition testimony and should be excluded. (D'Amico v. Board of Medical Examiners (1974) 11 Cal.3d 1, 22; Preach v. Monter Rainbow (1993) 12 Cal.App.4th 1441, 1451; Visueta v. General Motors Corp. (1991) 234 Cal.App.3d 1609.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Sandy Lee of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 971 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

RULING:
PLAINTIFF HAROLD SIMMONS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held. The Court ruled as follows: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted per CCP 36(a). Trial is August 12, 2013 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day for hearing summary motions is August 8, 2013. Summary motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant sections of the C.C.P. The time to respond to written discovery is shortened to 20 days. Plaintiff must serve responses to Standard Interrogatories, Set II, and all supplemental responses, by May 7. The parties must meet and confer on dates so that plaintiff's deposition concludes no later than June 3. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on all defendants no later than July 19, 2013. Responses to the demand are due no later than July 26, 2013. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on August 8, 2013. Fact discovery cut-off date is July 26, 2013. Expert discovery cut off is August 9, 2013. No later than May 8, 2013, plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than May 8, 2013, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. All parties to meet and confer regarding any other scheduling or other issue which any of them want to have included in the preference order. Further case management conference on June 19, 2013 at 1:30pm in Dept. 503. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to S. F. Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on August 12, 2013 at 1:30 pm in Dept. 503. Parties shall comply with Local Rule 5 and provide confidential settlement statements by August 8, 2013 at 2:00 p.m. Plaintiff to submit an order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Miles Lendway of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13684 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
